one_line_core: AI Agent领域正经历从能力扩张到系统化成熟的范式转变，核心矛盾从“能否执行任务”转向“能否被信任和高效部署”，催生了在调试、推理、平台和商业化层面的系统性解决方案与新的张力。
executive_summary: AI Agent生态正从追求单一能力突破，转向解决规模化、可信赖部署的系统性挑战。微软的AgentRx框架和vLLM项目分别从可调试性和推理效率两个底层维度，试图弥合Agent能力增长与可控性、资源消耗之间的根本矛盾。
市场对专业化、高性能Agent（如Qwen的编码智能体）需求旺盛，但验证其真实能力与平衡开源生态成为新挑战。同时，Claude Opus 4.7等顶级模型的迭代，以及Cloudflare等平台推出专用推理层，标志着Agent基础设施竞赛的升级。
伴随商业化的深入，新的矛盾开始浮现，例如Laravel案例所暗示的商业模式（如广告注入）与用户体验、开发者信任之间的潜在冲突，预示着Agent生态在追求成熟的同时，需谨慎处理增长与可持续性的关系。

key_points.title: 可调试性成为Agent信任与部署的关键瓶颈
key_points.why_it_matters: 随着AI Agent承担更复杂、关键的任务（如云事故管理），其决策过程的不透明性（“黑箱”）使得诊断和修复失败变得极其困难。微软推出AgentRx框架，旨在系统化解决这一“调试鸿沟”，这是将Agent从实验性工具推向企业级可靠应用必须跨越的门槛。

key_points.title: 推理效率是Agent规模化应用的底层制约
key_points.why_it_matters: vLLM等项目通过系统级创新（如PagedAttention）优化LLM推理的吞吐和内存效率，直接应对模型能力膨胀与硬件算力、成本限制的根本矛盾。高效的推理引擎是海量Agent能够经济、实时运行的基础设施前提。

key_points.title: 专业化与开源化并行，市场验证决定长期价值
key_points.why_it_matters: Qwen推出专注于编码的智能体并开放访问，反映了市场对垂直领域高性能Agent的需求。其高热度表明技术社区的期待，但“宣称的Agentic能力”与“广泛、多样化的真实场景验证”之间的矛盾，将是决定其影响力和开源模式成功与否的关键。

key_points.title: 基础设施平台化与顶级模型迭代加速生态竞争
key_points.why_it_matters: Cloudflare推出“为Agent设计的推理层”，以及Claude Opus 4.7的发布并获得极高关注，表明竞争已从单一模型能力，扩展到提供端到端Agent部署、运行优化的平台级解决方案。这加速了整个生态的成熟，但也提高了准入门槛。

key_points.title: 商业化探索引发新模式与用户体验的新张力
key_points.why_it_matters: Laravel被报道在Agent中注入广告的案例（需进一步验证），虽是个例信号，但揭示了Agent商业化进程中一个潜在的普遍矛盾：寻求盈利的新模式（如广告、服务订阅）可能与开发者体验、用户信任和Agent的核心价值主张产生冲突。

key_insights: AI Agent的发展阶段已进入“系统化成熟期”，核心矛盾从外部（Agent能否完成任务）转向内部（系统是否可靠、高效、可管理）。解决这些系统性挑战（调试、推理、部署）的价值，可能不亚于甚至超过单纯的模型能力提升。
Agent生态正在形成清晰的层次结构：底层是推理与调试引擎（如vLLM, AgentRx），中间是模型与专业化能力（如Claude, Qwen），上层是部署与服务平台（如Cloudflare）。竞争焦点正沿此链条扩散，成功需要在整个技术栈具备优势。
开源与商业化之间的张力在Agent领域尤为突出。开源能加速生态建设和验证（如Qwen），但如何在此基础上构建可持续的商业模式，同时避免损害开发者信任（如广告注入的潜在风险），是项目长期生存必须解答的难题。
高关注度（如Claude的高分）并不直接等同于技术突破或市场成功，它可能反映的是社区对行业领导者动向的密切关注，或是某种焦虑与期待的混合体。需要结合具体技术细节和后续采用情况来判断其真实影响。
limitations: 本摘要基于有限的初步主题分析列表，其中部分主题（如Cloudflare AI Platform, Laravel广告注入）证据深度不足，其核心主张和影响需要更多来源和细节进行交叉验证。
分析主要反映技术社区（如Hacker News）的关注点和视角，可能未充分涵盖企业级用户、非技术消费者或特定垂直行业的实际采用情况与挑战。
对于“矛盾”和“洞察”的提炼，基于输入的分析框架，可能受原分析者主观判断影响。不同分析者可能对同一事实的矛盾焦点和核心洞察有不同解读。
摘要未能深入每个主题的具体技术实现细节（如AgentRx的具体机制、vLLM的最新优化），因此对于技术选型或深度投资的指导意义有限。
actions: 对于技术决策者：应优先评估和引入能解决Agent系统性挑战（如调试、推理效率）的工具或框架（AgentRx, vLLM），而不仅仅是追逐模型能力的迭代，以构建可靠、可维护的Agent应用。
对于开发者与研究者：密切关注并参与验证像Qwen3.6-35B-A3B这类开源专业化Agent的实际性能，特别是在复杂、真实的编码工作流中，以辨别宣传与实效，并为生态贡献反馈。
对于投资者与战略规划者：关注Agent基础设施层（推理服务、调试工具、部署平台）的投资机会和竞争格局，该层面可能正在形成新的壁垒和赢家，其商业模式也需谨慎审视。
建议后续情报收集：加强对已验证的Agent商业化案例（包括收入模式和用户接受度）以及企业生产环境部署Agent时遇到的实际运维挑战（超越技术社区讨论）的信息挖掘。

facts: AI agents are transitioning from simple chatbots to autonomous systems capable of complex tasks like managing cloud incidents and executing multi-step workflows.
A key emerging challenge with these advanced AI agents is a lack of transparency and debuggability when they fail.
Failures can include hallucinating tool outputs or other errors where the internal logic is not traceable.
Microsoft Research is introducing a framework called AgentRx to address this systematic debugging challenge.
contradictions: Increasing AI agent autonomy and capability vs. the decreasing transparency and debuggability of their actions.
The need for reliable, complex AI systems in critical workflows vs. the current difficulty in diagnosing and fixing their failures.
The aspiration for AI agents to operate like human experts in troubleshooting vs. the fundamental opacity of their decision-making processes compared to humans.
analyses.primary_contradiction: Increasing AI agent autonomy and capability vs. the decreasing transparency and debuggability of their actions.
analyses.core_insight: The evolution of AI agents into autonomous executors of critical tasks has created a fundamental 'debuggability gap'—their growing complexity is inversely related to our ability to understand and correct their failures, which is a major barrier to trust and deployment in high-stakes scenarios.
analyses.title: Systematic debugging for AI agents: Introducing the AgentRx framework

facts: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B模型发布，专注于智能体编码能力，现已向所有人开放。
该主题在Hacker News上获得600分的高评分和292条评论，表明其在技术社区引发了高度关注和讨论。
模型定位属于AI-LLM-Agent领域，且被标记为热门和趋势性话题。
contradictions: 模型的高性能宣称与社区对其实际能力验证需求之间的矛盾。
开源/开放访问的普惠理念与维持技术领先性及商业可持续性之间的潜在张力。
专注于编码的Agent能力与通用AI助手广泛需求之间的定位矛盾。
analyses.primary_contradiction: 模型宣称的'Agentic coding power'（智能体编码能力）与社区大规模、多样化真实场景验证需求之间的矛盾。
analyses.core_insight: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B的高热度反映了市场对专业化、高性能开源编码智能体的强烈需求，但其长期影响力取决于能否在社区广泛使用中持续证明其宣称的Agentic能力，并平衡好开源生态与技术进步的关系。
analyses.title: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B: Agentic coding power, now open to all

facts: vLLM 是一个专为大型语言模型设计的高吞吐、内存高效的推理与服务引擎
项目标签显示其支持 CUDA、AMD、TPU、Blackwell 等多种硬件平台
标签列表表明 vLLM 已适配或关联 GPT、Llama、Qwen、DeepSeek-V3、Kimi 等多个主流与前沿 LLM 模型
项目核心定位是解决 LLM 推理与服务场景下的性能与资源瓶颈
contradictions: 模型规模持续增长与有限硬件计算/内存资源之间的矛盾
对高吞吐、低延迟推理的服务需求与硬件算力成本之间的矛盾
支持多样化硬件平台（CUDA/AMD/TPU）与保持统一高性能优化之间的矛盾
适配众多快速演进的 LLM 架构（如 MoE）与引擎通用性、稳定性维护之间的矛盾
开源生态的广泛适配需求与核心性能深度优化所需封闭性之间的矛盾
analyses.primary_contradiction: LLM 模型对计算与内存资源的巨大需求与硬件实际供给能力及成本约束之间的矛盾
analyses.core_insight: vLLM 的核心价值在于通过系统级创新（如 PagedAttention）在硬件资源约束下最大化 LLM 服务的吞吐与效率，其发展实质是持续调和 LLM 能力膨胀与硬件现实之间的根本矛盾。

forecast.best_case: 在短期内（3-6个月），AI Agent生态的系统性成熟加速。微软的AgentRx等调试框架被早期采用者验证，有效提升了复杂Agent任务的可追溯性和可靠性，增强了企业部署信心。vLLM等推理优化技术持续降低部署成本，推动更多实验性Agent应用进入小规模生产环境。专业化开源模型（如Qwen编码智能体）在社区驱动下，于特定场景（如辅助编程）中证明其价值，形成初步的开发者生态。基础设施平台（如Cloudflare）的专用层开始吸引开发者，整个技术栈的协同效应初步显现，推动Agent从演示和概念验证阶段向有限的、高价值的实际应用场景稳步过渡。
forecast.worst_case: 系统性挑战的解决速度慢于预期，导致短期发展遇冷。AgentRx等调试框架被证明在真实世界的复杂故障面前仍力有不逮，未能实质性解决“黑箱”问题，挫伤企业将Agent用于关键任务的积极性。推理效率的优化触及硬件瓶颈，成本下降不及预期，限制了Agent的规模化试验。开源专业化模型（如Qwen）在广泛社区测试中暴露出能力与宣传不符、或仅在特定基准上表现良好等问题，引发信任危机，减缓了开源生态的贡献和采用速度。同时，过早的商业化尝试（如广告注入模式）引发开发者强烈反弹，损害整个生态的信任基础。市场情绪从过热转向观望，投资和开发活动出现阶段性收缩。
forecast.most_likely_case: 短期（3-6个月）内，AI Agent领域将呈现“分层演进、热点分散”的格局。底层基础设施（如vLLM的持续优化）和关键痛点解决方案（如AgentRx的初步发布与概念验证）会获得技术社区的持续关注和讨论，但大规模生产级应用仍为少数。像Claude Opus 4.7这样的顶级模型迭代会引发高度关注和性能评测热潮，但其对Agent生态的实际推动需要更长时间显现。Qwen等开源专业化Agent会在特定开发者圈子内引发试用和评测，形成小范围的口碑，但难以在短期内颠覆现有工具链。Cloudflare等平台的新功能会吸引早期尝鲜者，但形成稳定工作流尚需时日。整体上，行业处于“系统性能力建设期”，而非“应用爆发期”。商业化与用户体验的冲突（如广告案例）会作为警示性话题被讨论，但不会成为主流模式。市场将保持谨慎乐观，重点从追逐新模型发布，转向验证现有工具链的可靠性和经济性。
key_variables: AgentRx等调试工具在真实复杂场景下的实际效果验证
vLLM等推理引擎对新模型（特别是MoE架构）的适配效率与性能提升
Qwen3.6-35B-A3B等开源专业化模型在社区广泛测试后的真实口碑与漏洞发现
主流云平台（AWS, GCP, Azure）对Agent专用推理/部署基础设施的跟进策略与定价
开发者社区对Agent商业化模式（尤其是侵入式广告）的接受度与反弹程度
forecast.forecast_conclusion: 基于当前信息，短期（3-6个月）内AI Agent领域最可能的发展路径是“夯实基础，而非全面开花”。核心矛盾（能力与可控性、效率）的解决方案（AgentRx, vLLM）将处于关键的早期验证期，其成功与否将决定中期发展速度。市场热点会同时存在于基础设施优化、顶级模型迭代和专业化开源项目上，但任何单一突破都难以立即引发生态级变革。行业整体将朝着系统化、工程化方向演进，同时谨慎试探商业化的边界。

signals.title: PlugMem: Transforming raw agent interactions into reusable knowledge
signals.summary:
It seems counterintuitive: giving AI agents more memory can make them less effective. As interaction logs accumulate, they grow large, fill with irrelevant content, and become increasingly difficult to use. More memory means that agents must search through larger volumes of past interactions to find information relevant to the current task. Without structure, these records mix […]

The post PlugMem: Transforming raw agent interactions into reusable knowledge appeared first on Microsoft Research.

signals.title: From Feelings to Metrics: Understanding and Formalizing How Users Vibe-Test LLMs
signals.summary: Evaluating LLMs is challenging, as benchmark scores often fail to capture models' real-world usefulness. Instead, users often rely on ``vibe-testing'': informal experience-based evaluation, such as comparing models on coding tasks related to their own workflow. While prevalent, vibe-testing is often too ad hoc and unstructured to analyze or reproduce at scale. In this work, we study how vibe-testing works in practice and then formalize it to support systematic analysis. We first analyze two empirical resources: (1) a survey of user evaluation practices, and (2) a collection of in-the-wild model comparison reports from blogs and social media. Based on these resources, we formalize vibe-testing as a two-part process: users personalize both what they test and how they judge responses. We then introduce a proof-of-concept evaluation pipeline that follows this formulation by generating personalized prompts and comparing model outputs using user-aware subjective criteria. In experiments on coding benchmarks, we find that combining personalized prompts and user-aware evaluation can change which model is preferred, reflecting the role of vibe-testing in practice. These findings suggest that formalized vibe-testing can serve as a useful approach for bridging benchmark scores and real-world experience.

signals.title: Rhetorical Questions in LLM Representations: A Linear Probing Study
signals.summary: Rhetorical questions are asked not to seek information but to persuade or signal stance. How large language models internally represent them remains unclear. We analyze rhetorical questions in LLM representations using linear probes on two social-media datasets with different discourse contexts, and find that rhetorical signals emerge early and are most stably captured by last-token representations. Rhetorical questions are linearly separable from information-seeking questions within datasets, and remain detectable under cross-dataset transfer, reaching AUROC around 0.7-0.8. However, we demonstrate that transferability does not simply imply a shared representation. Probes trained on different datasets produce different rankings when applied to the same target corpus, with overlap among the top-ranked instances often below 0.2. Qualitative analysis shows that these divergences correspond to distinct rhetorical phenomena: some probes capture discourse-level rhetorical stance embedded in extended argumentation, while others emphasize localized, syntax-driven interrogative acts. Together, these findings suggest that rhetorical questions in LLM representations are encoded by multiple linear directions emphasizing different cues, rather than a single shared direction.

signals.title: TREX: Automating LLM Fine-tuning via Agent-Driven Tree-based Exploration
signals.summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have empowered AI research agents to perform isolated scientific tasks, automating complex, real-world workflows, such as LLM training, remains a significant challenge. In this paper, we introduce TREX, a multi-agent system that automates the entire LLM training life-cycle. By orchestrating collaboration between two core modules-the Researcher and the Executor-the system seamlessly performs requirement analysis, open-domain literature and data research, formulation of training strategies, preparation of data recipes, and model training and evaluation. The multi-round experimental process is modeled as a search tree, enabling the system to efficiently plan exploration paths, reuse historical results, and distill high-level insights from iterative trials. To evaluate the capability of automated LLM training, we construct FT-Bench, a benchmark comprising 10 tasks derived from real-world scenarios, ranging from optimizing fundamental model capabilities to enhancing performance on domain-specific tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that the TREX agent consistently optimizes model performance on target tasks.

signals.title: LongCoT: Benchmarking Long-Horizon Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
signals.summary: As language models are increasingly deployed for complex autonomous tasks, their ability to reason accurately over longer horizons becomes critical. An essential component of this ability is planning and managing a long, complex chain-of-thought (CoT). We introduce LongCoT, a scalable benchmark of 2,500 expert-designed problems spanning chemistry, mathematics, computer science, chess, and logic to isolate and directly measure the long-horizon CoT reasoning capabilities of frontier models. Problems consist of a short input with a verifiable answer; solving them requires navigating a graph of interdependent steps that span tens to hundreds of thousands of reasoning tokens. Each local step is individually tractable for frontier models, so failures reflect long-horizon reasoning limitations. At release, the best models achieve <10% accuracy (GPT 5.2: 9.8%; Gemini 3 Pro: 6.1%) on LongCoT, revealing a substantial gap in current capabilities. Overall, LongCoT provides a rigorous measure of long-horizon reasoning, tracking the ability of frontier models to reason reliably over extended periods.

support_evidence.title: Strategic Response of News Publishers to Generative AI
support_evidence.summary: Generative AI can adversely impact news publishers by lowering consumer demand. It can also reduce demand for newsroom employees, and increase the creation of news "slop." However, it can also form a source of traffic referrals and an information-discovery channel that increases demand. We use high-frequency granular data to analyze the strategic response of news publishers to the introduction of Generative AI. Many publishers strategically blocked LLM access to their websites using the robots.txt file standard. Using a difference-in-differences approach, we find that large publishers who block GenAI bots experience reduced website traffic compared to not blocking. In addition, we find that large publishers shift toward richer content that is harder for LLMs to replicate, without increasing text volume. Finally, we find that the share of new editorial and content-production job postings rises over time. Together, these findings illustrate the levers that publishers choose to use to strategically respond to competitive Generative AI threats, and their consequences.

support_evidence.title: HiVLA: A Visual-Grounded-Centric Hierarchical Embodied Manipulation System
support_evidence.summary: While end-to-end Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models offer a promising paradigm for robotic manipulation, fine-tuning them on narrow control data often compromises the profound reasoning capabilities inherited from their base Vision-Language Models (VLMs). To resolve this fundamental trade-off, we propose HiVLA, a visual-grounded-centric hierarchical framework that explicitly decouples high-level semantic planning from low-level motor control. In high-level part, a VLM planner first performs task decomposition and visual grounding to generate structured plans, comprising a subtask instruction and a precise target bounding box. Then, to translate this plan into physical actions, we introduce a flow-matching Diffusion Transformer (DiT) action expert in low-level part equipped with a novel cascaded cross-attention mechanism. This design sequentially fuses global context, high-resolution object-centric crops and skill semantics, enabling the DiT to focus purely on robust execution. Our decoupled architecture preserves the VLM's zero-shot reasoning while allowing independent improvement of both components. Extensive experiments in simulation and the real world demonstrate that HiVLA significantly outperforms state-of-the-art end-to-end baselines, particularly excelling in long-horizon skill composition and the fine-grained manipulation of small objects in cluttered scenes.

support_evidence.title: LongCoT: Benchmarking Long-Horizon Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
support_evidence.summary: As language models are increasingly deployed for complex autonomous tasks, their ability to reason accurately over longer horizons becomes critical. An essential component of this ability is planning and managing a long, complex chain-of-thought (CoT). We introduce LongCoT, a scalable benchmark of 2,500 expert-designed problems spanning chemistry, mathematics, computer science, chess, and logic to isolate and directly measure the long-horizon CoT reasoning capabilities of frontier models. Problems consist of a short input with a verifiable answer; solving them requires navigating a graph of interdependent steps that span tens to hundreds of thousands of reasoning tokens. Each local step is individually tractable for frontier models, so failures reflect long-horizon reasoning limitations. At release, the best models achieve <10% accuracy (GPT 5.2: 9.8%; Gemini 3 Pro: 6.1%) on LongCoT, revealing a substantial gap in current capabilities. Overall, LongCoT provides a rigorous measure of long-horizon reasoning, tracking the ability of frontier models to reason reliably over extended periods.

signals.title: From $P(y|x)$ to $P(y)$: Investigating Reinforcement Learning in Pre-train Space
signals.summary: While reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) significantly enhances LLM reasoning by optimizing the conditional distribution P(y|x), its potential is fundamentally bounded by the base model's existing output distribution. Optimizing the marginal distribution P(y) in the Pre-train Space addresses this bottleneck by encoding reasoning ability and preserving broad exploration capacity. Yet, conventional pre-training relies on static corpora for passive learning, leading to a distribution shift that hinders targeted reasoning enhancement. In this paper, we introduce PreRL (Pre-train Space RL), which applies reward-driven online updates directly to P(y). We theoretically and empirically validate the strong gradient alignment between log P(y) and log P(y|x), establishing PreRL as a viable surrogate for standard RL. Furthermore, we uncover a critical mechanism: Negative Sample Reinforcement (NSR) within PreRL serves as an exceptionally effective driver for reasoning. NSR-PreRL rapidly prunes incorrect reasoning spaces while stimulating endogenous reflective behaviors, increasing transition and reflection thoughts by 14.89x and 6.54x, respectively. Leveraging these insights, we propose Dual Space RL (DSRL), a Policy Reincarnation strategy that initializes models with NSR-PreRL to expand the reasoning horizon before transitioning to standard RL for fine-grained optimization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DSRL consistently outperforms strong baselines, proving that pre-train space pruning effectively steers the policy toward a refined correct reasoning subspace.

support_evidence.title: Claude is a space to think

signals.title: Exact Structural Abstraction and Tractability Limits
signals.summary:
Any rigorously specified problem determines an admissible-output relation $R$, and the only state distinctions that matter are the classes $s \sim_R s' \iff \mathrm{Adm}_R(s)=\mathrm{Adm}_R(s')$. Every exact correctness claim reduces to the same quotient-recovery problem, and the no-go concerns tractability of the underlying problem, not of its presentation. Exact means agreement with $R$, not zero-error determinism or absence of approximation/randomization in the specification. The exact-semantics quotient theorem identifies admissible-output equivalence as the canonical object recovered by exact relevance certification. Decision, search, approximation, statistical, randomized, horizon, and distributional guarantees instantiate it. Tractable families have a finite primitive basis, but optimizer-quotient realizability is maximal, so quotient shape cannot characterize the frontier.
  We prove a meta-impossibility theorem for efficiently checkable structural predicates invariant under theorem-forced closure laws of exact certification. Zero-distortion summaries, quotient entropy bounds, and support counting explain them. Same-orbit disagreements across four obstruction families, via action-independent pair-targeted affine witnesses, force contradiction. Consequently no correct problem-tractability classifier on a closure-closed domain yields an exact characterization over these families. Restricting to a closure-closed subdomain helps only by removing orbit gaps. Uniform strict-gap control preserves the full optimizer quotient, while arbitrarily small perturbations can flip relevance and sufficiency. Closure-orbit agreement is forced by correctness, and the same compute-cost barrier extends to optimizer computation, payload/search, and theorem-backed external or transported outputs. The obstruction therefore appears at the level of correctness itself, not any particular output formalism.

This design sequentially fuses global context, high-resolution object-centric crops and skill semantics, enabling the DiT to focus purely on robust execution. Our decoupled architecture preserves the VLM's zero-shot reasoning while allowing independent improvement of both components. Extensive experiments in simulation and the real world demonstrate that HiVLA significantly outperforms state-of-the-art end-to-end baselines, particularly excelling in long-horizon skill composition and the fine-grained manipulation of small objects in cluttered scenes.

Vision-language models (VLMs) use images and text to plan robot actions, but they still struggle to decide what actions to take and where to take them. Most systems split these decisions into two steps: a VLM generates a plan in natural language, and a separate model translates it into executable actions. This approach often breaks […]

The post GroundedPlanBench: Spatially grounded long-horizon task planning for robot manipulation appeared first on Microsoft Research.

signals.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
signals.summary: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succeeds or fails: (i) the student and teacher should share compatible thinking patterns; and (ii) even with consistent thinking patterns and higher scores, the teacher must offer genuinely new capabilities beyond what the student has seen during training. We validate these findings through weak-to-strong reverse distillation, showing that same-family 1.5B and 7B teachers are distributionally indistinguishable from the student's perspective. Probing into the token-level mechanism, we show that successful OPD is characterized by progressive alignment on high-probability tokens at student-visited states, a small shared token set that concentrates most of the probability mass (97%-99%). We further propose two practical strategies to recover failing OPD: off-policy cold start and teacher-aligned prompt selection. Finally, we show that OPD's apparent free lunch of dense token-level reward comes at a cost, raising the question of whether OPD can scale to long-horizon distillation.

support_evidence.title: Getting Started with Edge AI on NVIDIA Jetson: LLMs, VLMs, and Foundation Models for Robotics

signals.title: Strategic Response of News Publishers to Generative AI
signals.summary: Generative AI can adversely impact news publishers by lowering consumer demand. It can also reduce demand for newsroom employees, and increase the creation of news "slop." However, it can also form a source of traffic referrals and an information-discovery channel that increases demand. We use high-frequency granular data to analyze the strategic response of news publishers to the introduction of Generative AI. Many publishers strategically blocked LLM access to their websites using the robots.txt file standard. Using a difference-in-differences approach, we find that large publishers who block GenAI bots experience reduced website traffic compared to not blocking. In addition, we find that large publishers shift toward richer content that is harder for LLMs to replicate, without increasing text volume. Finally, we find that the share of new editorial and content-production job postings rises over time. Together, these findings illustrate the levers that publishers choose to use to strategically respond to competitive Generative AI threats, and their consequences.

support_evidence.title: Anthropic’s Long-Term Benefit Trust appoints Vas Narasimhan to Board of Directors
Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
New Future of Work: AI is driving rapid change, uneven benefits
support_evidence.summary:
For the past five years, the New Future of Work report has captured how work is changing. This year, the shift feels especially sharp. Previous editions have focused on technology’s role in increasing productivity by automating tasks, accelerating communication, and expanding access to information, as well as the rise of remote work. Today, generative AI […]

The post New Future of Work: AI is driving rapid change, uneven benefits appeared first on Microsoft Research.

signals.title: UI-Zoomer: Uncertainty-Driven Adaptive Zoom-In for GUI Grounding
signals.summary: GUI grounding, which localizes interface elements from screenshots given natural language queries, remains challenging for small icons and dense layouts. Test-time zoom-in methods improve localization by cropping and re-running inference at higher resolution, but apply cropping uniformly across all instances with fixed crop sizes, ignoring whether the model is actually uncertain on each case. We propose \textbf{UI-Zoomer}, a training-free adaptive zoom-in framework that treats both the trigger and scale of zoom-in as a prediction uncertainty quantification problem. A confidence-aware gate fuses spatial consensus among stochastic candidates with token-level generation confidence to selectively trigger zoom-in only when localization is uncertain. When triggered, an uncertainty-driven crop sizing module decomposes prediction variance into inter-sample positional spread and intra-sample box extent, deriving a per-instance crop radius via the law of total variance. Extensive experiments on ScreenSpot-Pro, UI-Vision, and ScreenSpot-v2 demonstrate consistent improvements over strong baselines across multiple model architectures, achieving gains of up to +13.4\%, +10.3\%, and +4.2\% respectively, with no additional training required.

support_evidence.title: From $P(y|x)$ to $P(y)$: Investigating Reinforcement Learning in Pre-train Space
support_evidence.summary: While reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) significantly enhances LLM reasoning by optimizing the conditional distribution P(y|x), its potential is fundamentally bounded by the base model's existing output distribution. Optimizing the marginal distribution P(y) in the Pre-train Space addresses this bottleneck by encoding reasoning ability and preserving broad exploration capacity. Yet, conventional pre-training relies on static corpora for passive learning, leading to a distribution shift that hinders targeted reasoning enhancement. In this paper, we introduce PreRL (Pre-train Space RL), which applies reward-driven online updates directly to P(y). We theoretically and empirically validate the strong gradient alignment between log P(y) and log P(y|x), establishing PreRL as a viable surrogate for standard RL. Furthermore, we uncover a critical mechanism: Negative Sample Reinforcement (NSR) within PreRL serves as an exceptionally effective driver for reasoning. NSR-PreRL rapidly prunes incorrect reasoning spaces while stimulating endogenous reflective behaviors, increasing transition and reflection thoughts by 14.89x and 6.54x, respectively. Leveraging these insights, we propose Dual Space RL (DSRL), a Policy Reincarnation strategy that initializes models with NSR-PreRL to expand the reasoning horizon before transitioning to standard RL for fine-grained optimization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DSRL consistently outperforms strong baselines, proving that pre-train space pruning effectively steers the policy toward a refined correct reasoning subspace.

support_evidence.title: Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute
LongCoT: Benchmarking Long-Horizon Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
support_evidence.summary: As language models are increasingly deployed for complex autonomous tasks, their ability to reason accurately over longer horizons becomes critical. An essential component of this ability is planning and managing a long, complex chain-of-thought (CoT). We introduce LongCoT, a scalable benchmark of 2,500 expert-designed problems spanning chemistry, mathematics, computer science, chess, and logic to isolate and directly measure the long-horizon CoT reasoning capabilities of frontier models. Problems consist of a short input with a verifiable answer; solving them requires navigating a graph of interdependent steps that span tens to hundreds of thousands of reasoning tokens. Each local step is individually tractable for frontier models, so failures reflect long-horizon reasoning limitations. At release, the best models achieve <10% accuracy (GPT 5.2: 9.8%; Gemini 3 Pro: 6.1%) on LongCoT, revealing a substantial gap in current capabilities. Overall, LongCoT provides a rigorous measure of long-horizon reasoning, tracking the ability of frontier models to reason reliably over extended periods.

We use high-frequency granular data to analyze the strategic response of news publishers to the introduction of Generative AI. predictions: 基于当前信息，短期（3-6个月）内AI Agent领域最可能的发展路径是“夯实基础，而非全面开花”。核心矛盾（能力与可控性、效率）的解决方案（AgentRx, vLLM）将处于关键的早期验证期，其成功与否将决定中期发展速度。市场热点会同时存在于基础设施优化、顶级模型迭代和专业化开源项目上，但任何单一突破都难以立即引发生态级变革。行业整体将朝着系统化、工程化方向演进，同时谨慎试探商业化的边界。
